title: Redirect URI doesn’t work with sub domain
excerpt: when I try to use monday oauth domains like “app.domain.com” returns error when I put them in the redirect URI. also had problem with domain.com/app. but domain.com works. Is there a way to solve this problem?

posts.author: idojet (Ido avidan)
posts.cookedHtml: when I try to use monday oauth domains like “app.domain.com” returns error when I put them in the redirect URI. also had problem with domain.com/app. but domain.com works. Is there a way to solve this problem?

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @idojet,

Let me check in with the team on this one - I’ll post again as soon as I have an answer for you.

-Daniel

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @idojet,

Thanks for letting us know about this! I’ve gone ahead reported this to our developers so that they are aware of this - they’ll look into this further on their end, and work on fixing it as soon as they are able to.

However I am not able to provide an ETA for the resolution at the moment as we’ll need to further review this and schedule accordingly. This being said - I’ll post again on here as soon as I have more news on this.

-Daniel

posts.author: mitchell.hudson (Mitchell hudson)
posts.cookedHtml:
@idojet

What error are you receiving?

We use subdomains for our app redirects in all of our apps and haven’t seen an issue.

For example: https://appname.subdomain.domain/monday/callback and this works fine.
